task_completion.description: Verify that a new case named 'Litigation_Support_2024' with Case Number 'INV-EDISC-01' was created in Autopsy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Autopsy main window title bar, case summary dashboard, or case creation dialog showing 'Litigation_Support_2024' and 'INV-EDISC-01'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the disk image 'ntfs_undel.dd' was added as a data source to the case.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'ntfs_undel.dd' in the Autopsy tree view on the left (under Data Sources) or in a data source summary window.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Hash Lookup and File Type Identification modules were configured or run.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Run Ingest Modules' dialog showing checkboxes ticked for 'Hash Lookup' and 'File Type Identification', or progress bars/results indicating these specific modules were executed.

task_completion.description: Verify that allocated user files were extracted to the Natives directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager window or terminal listing files inside '/home/ga/Reports/eDiscovery/Natives/'. The files should appear to be actual extracted user files (not directories or system metafiles like $MFT).

task_completion.description: Verify that the pipe-delimited load file was generated with the correct headers and metadata.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor, terminal output (e.g., cat command), or script showing the contents of '/home/ga/Reports/eDiscovery/load_file.dat'. It must contain the exact header 'CONTROL_NUMBER|FILENAME|ORIGINAL_PATH|SIZE_BYTES|MIME_TYPE|MD5_HASH|EXPORT_PATH' and subsequent rows with pipe-delimited (|) metadata.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used Autopsy to process the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the Autopsy GUI open and being interacted with, rather than just a Python script bypassing the forensic software entirely.

integrity.description: Ensure the extracted files and load file contain genuine data from the disk image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The load file contents should show realistic MD5 hashes, MIME types, and file sizes corresponding to the extracted files, not placeholder text or dummy values.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not just manually type a fake load file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of a script or Autopsy export feature being used to generate the load file programmatically based on the case data, rather than manual text entry of a few fake rows.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Case Name: 'Litigation_Support_2024'. Target Case Number: 'INV-EDISC-01'. Disk Image Path: '/home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd'. Export Directory: '/home/ga/Reports/eDiscovery/Natives'. Load File Path: '/home/ga/Reports/eDiscovery/load_file.dat'. The load file must use pipe (|) delimiters and include the exact header: CONTROL_NUMBER|FILENAME|ORIGINAL_PATH|SIZE_BYTES|MIME_TYPE|MD5_HASH|EXPORT_PATH.
